summary: Excellent soundboard capture with Phil’s bass front‑and‑center, featuring a smoking Truckin’ jam, soaring China Cat Sunflower, epic Weather Report improv and a killer piano solo on Promised Land.
review: The recording is a crisp soundboard source that puts Phil Lesh solidly in the mix, delivering clear instrument separation. The show itself is hailed as one of ’74’s finest – highlighted by a monster Truckin’ jam, chilling China Cat Sunflower, an inspired Weather Report Part One, and a blistering Promised Land with Keith Godchaux’s soaring piano. All cylinders were firing, making this a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews are five‑star and uniformly praise both performance and sound quality; no criticisms were noted, indicating an exceptional show captured on a high‑quality soundboard.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Phil’s bass is prominently in the mix; overall clarity and balance are praised with no reported dropouts or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows through high‑energy psychedelic jams and tight song transitions, with the band fully psychedelized and delivering seamless improvisations.

band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is up in the mix and noted as a highlight of the recording.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob Weir) delivers standout guitar work on Promised Land and China Cat Sunflower.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux launches an epic piano solo right after “hit it Keith” in Promised Land.
